name: Fuente Cobre Trail
name_original: Nacimiento del río Pisuerga por la Senda de Fuente Cobre (PR-P-8) — circular por el Parque Natural Montaña Palentina
description: Hike the moderate 7.3-mile Fuente Cobre Trail in Montaña Palentina Natural Park to discover the traditional source of the Pisuerga River.
region_intro: Hiking around La Pernía offers access to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, characterized by high-mountain landscapes and glacial origins. The region features impressive peaks such as Pico Tres Mares and Peña Labra, alongside verdant valleys and the source of the Pisuerga River. These diverse natural features provide varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around La Pernía The most popular hiking route is Fuente Cobre Trail, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the traditional source of the Pisuerga River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares from the collado de Fuente del Chivo, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail offers views of significant mountain peaks. Local hikers also love the Senda del Bosque Fósil desde Verdeña (PRC-P 9) — circular por el Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a 2.3 miles (3.7 km) trail leading through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Hiking around La Pernía is defined by high-mountain landscapes, verdant valleys, and significant river systems. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in La Pernía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore La Pernía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Pernía?
A: La Pernía offers nearly 50 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find around 11 easy routes, 25 moderate, and 12 more challenging options.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in La Pernía?
A: Yes, La Pernía has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067839724/senda-del-bosque-fosil-desde-verdena-prc-p-9-circular-por-el-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Bosque Fósil desde Verdeña (PRC-P 9)&lt;/a&gt;, a circular route through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, which is about 2.3 miles (3.7 km) long and takes just over an hour to complete.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in La Pernía?
A: La Pernía is rich in diverse natural features. You can explore high-mountain landscapes, verdant valleys, and significant river systems. Highlights include impressive peaks like Pico Tres Mares and Peña Labra, and the traditional source of the Pisuerga River, which you can visit via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068162568/fuente-cobre-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Cobre Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features glacial origins and lush forests.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas along the trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its breathtaking panoramas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, located over 1,300 meters above sea level, offers spectacular vistas of the Picos de Europa, Peña Labra, Tres Mares, and the Liébana and Valdeprado Valleys. Several trails, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996923174/pena-labra-and-pico-tres-mares-from-the-collado-de-fuente-del-chivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares from the collado de Fuente del Chivo&lt;/a&gt;, also provide excellent mountain views.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in La Pernía?
A: Yes, circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067839724/senda-del-bosque-fosil-desde-verdena-prc-p-9-circular-por-el-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Bosque Fósil desde Verdeña (PRC-P 9)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Pernía?
A: The best time for hiking in La Pernía generally spans from late spring through early autumn (May to October), when the weather is milder and trails are most accessible. During winter, higher elevations may experience snow, offering different challenges and scenic beauty for those prepared for winter conditions.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in La Pernía?
A: While many trails in natural parks across Spain are dog-friendly, specific regulations can vary within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Check local signage for any specific restrictions on individual trails.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in La Pernía?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning high-mountain landscapes, the tranquility of the verdant valleys, and the opportunity to explore significant natural landmarks like the source of the Pisuerga River.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, La Pernía is also known for its rich Romanesque architecture. You can find historic sites like the Collegiate Church of San Salvador de Cantamuda, which adds a cultural dimension to your outdoor exploration. The region is also dotted with traditional mountain villages, offering a glimpse into local history.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in La Pernía?
A: La Pernía is known as a 'land of bears,' indicating the presence of diverse wildlife. Hikers might spot various species of birds, deer, and other mammals. The relatively untouched natural environment makes it an appealing destination for nature lovers, but always remember to observe wildlife from a respectful distance.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, La Pernía offers challenging routes for experienced hikers. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068176113/pico-tres-mares-y-pena-labra-desde-collado-de-piedrasluengas-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tres Mares y Peña Labra desde collado de Piedrasluengas&lt;/a&gt; involve significant elevation gain and cover over 12.8 km, providing a strenuous but rewarding experience with impressive views.

Q: Where can I find accommodation or places to eat after a hike?
A: The region has small mountain villages where you can find accommodation and places to eat. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2076803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is a nearby settlement that offers amenities. You can also find various huts and shelters, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3394566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Golobar Refuge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5144390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue de Villaverde&lt;/a&gt;, which might provide options for rest and sustenance.
tips: The Pisuerga River rises in the province of Palencia, in the region of Castile and León, Spain. Its source is in the Covarrés glacial basin, between the Valdecebollas peak and the Sierra de Híjar, in a place known as Sel de la Fuente.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts near Santa María de Redondo, where you can find parking facilities. The route itself begins close to remnants of old coal mining operations, marking the historical industrial past of the area.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Fuente Cobre Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it is located within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife, which includes brown bears, wild boars, and deer.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The ideal time to hike is from late spring through autumn. During winter and early spring, snow and water can make sections of the trail difficult to access, potentially requiring specialized equipment like snowshoes. Summer and early autumn offer pleasant conditions and vibrant scenery.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for the Montaña Palentina Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits required or entrance fees to hike the Fuente Cobre Trail within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. However, visitors are expected to respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse terrain, starting through forests of beech, oak, and hazelnut, sometimes traversing dry stream beds. As you ascend towards the Fuente del Cobre, the path becomes rockier with limestone crags. If you take the alternative return route, expect meadows and hillside paths with expansive views.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The main highlight is the Pisuerga River Source (Cueva del Cobre), where the river dramatically emerges from a cave. You'll also encounter numerous waterfalls, centenarian forests, and from higher points like the Collacoble meadows, panoramic views of the Cantabrian mountains, including Valdecebollas and Peña Tejedo.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I spot on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. Keep an eye out for brown bears, wild boars, deer, roe deer, choughs, rock thrushes, vultures, and Egyptian vultures. The area is a vital refuge for many species.

faqs.question: Is it a loop trail, or do I return the same way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers flexibility. You can return along the same path, making it an out-and-back route, or opt for an alternative semi-circular route. The circular option provides different perspectives and views of the valley and surrounding peaks on your return journey.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, good physical fitness and sure-footedness are recommended. Bring appropriate hiking footwear, water, snacks, and layers of clothing suitable for mountain weather. If hiking in winter or early spring, be prepared for snow and potentially specialized equipment like snowshoes.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants nearby after the hike?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself doesn't have facilities, you can find cafes, pubs, and restaurants in nearby villages such as Santa María de Redondo, which is a common starting point for the hike. These establishments offer a chance to refuel and relax after your adventure.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is primarily known as the Senda de Fuente Cobre, and you will be following sections of this well-signposted path throughout your hike.
